key_insight: The paper replaces overlapping FE–neural operator coupling with a non-overlapping Schwarz scheme that exchanges Neumann/Dirichlet data, enabling faster and more geometry-flexible hybrid solid mechanics simulation.
engineering_relevance: This makes high-fidelity simulation more practical for domains with local nonlinearities, fine-scale features, and irregular geometries, while preserving mechanical consistency.
method: Non-overlapping Schwarz alternating coupling between FE and Point-DeepONet subdomains
Neumann-Dirichlet interface exchange: traction from NO to FE, displacement from FE to NO
Analytical derivation of strain and stress operators from displacement to reduce parameters and enforce physics
evidence_signal: The framework is validated on three benchmarks—static linear elasticity, quasi-static hyperelasticity, and elastodynamics—covering both regular and irregular geometries, with reduced inner Schwarz iterations and bounded error accumulation reported in the abstract.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable interface coupling and accurate traction transfer; implementing the Neumann-Dirichlet exchange on complex meshes may be sensitive to discretization and training quality.
paper_title: A Non-Overlapping Schwarz Hybrid Finite Element-Neural Operator Framework for Solid Mechanics on Irregular Domains
